_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of the specific missions for CMF (i.e.,  Cyber Mission Teams (CMT), National Mission Teams (NMT), Combat Support Team (CST), National Support Team (NST), Cyber Protection Team (CPT).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of MLOps principles and practices, including CI/CD for ML, automated retraining pipelines, model versioning, A/B testing infrastructure, and canary deployment patterns.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-K-017 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.476), AI-K-023 (AI, cosine: 0.456).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.6/10 (raw 0.265). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [cmf, cmt, combat, cpt, cst, cyber] vs WIDAI-only terms [automated, b, canary, cd, ci, deployment] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.806 (P=0.790, R=0.823). R exceeds P by 0.032 — the WIDAI KSA's 22 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 28 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.9%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of the specific missions for CMF (i.e.,  Cyber Mission Teams (CMT), Na" and "Knowledge of MLOps principles and practices, including CI/CD for ML, automated r". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.476 > STS 0.265 (gap +0.211). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.1%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [cmf, cmt, combat, cpt, cst, cyber]. WIDAI-only: [automated, b, canary, cd, ci, deployment].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.9% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.211 with low STS 0.265. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
